mechanism_narrative: A declining federal funds rate over the trailing 3 months signals monetary easing, which lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and lifts forward S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and 20d forward returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns following declining-rate periods are not significantly higher than the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window during a late-cycle expansion, it signals an impending labor-market turn that historically precedes equity drawdowns, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: In more than 50% of historical trigger instances during late-cycle regimes, 60-day forward returns are positive, or the Sahm-style trigger fails to precede a return decline of statistically significant magnitude (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) reflects broad-based consumer and investment demand strength that jointly forecasts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month, whereas either series alone is a weak predictor.
failure_condition: The joint RSAFS-HOUST interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over either univariate series alone (interaction coefficient not significant at p < 0.10) in walk-forward regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: A rising trailing 3-month change in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects strengthening manufacturing output and corporate earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week as markets price in improved near-term profitability.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO 3-month momentum and 5-day forward returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 1-year window, indicating no exploitable short-horizon signal.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, when FEDFUNDS is rising alongside persistently elevated CPIAUCSL inflation, the market anticipates further restrictive policy, compressing valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter; in non-tightening regimes this interaction has no predictive value.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rising for 3 consecutive months AND CPIAUCSL YoY > 3%
failure_condition: In periods satisfying the joint trigger, 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction term is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) exceeding a threshold signals labor market deterioration that precedes reduced consumer spending and corporate revenue, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following a negative PAYEMS print are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or the effect is not significant at p > 0.05 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) signals durable economic expansion that supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or high-revision noise in GDPC1 eliminates the observed relationship when using real-time vintages.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in CPIAUCSL month-over-month inflation combined with a federal funds rate that has not yet adjusted upward signals a policy lag that markets interpret as forcing a future rate shock, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week as risk premia reprice.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL MoM change > 0.5% AND FEDFUNDS unchanged for 2+ months
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following the joint trigger are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or the interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) relative to a CPIAUCSL-only baseline model.
